name: Cruz de la Atalaya Trail
name_original: Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas — circular desde Fitero
description: Hike the 8.9-mile Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas circular from Fitero, a moderate route with historical sites and views.
region_intro: Hiking around Fitero offers diverse experiences across varied terrain in the Ribera de Navarra region. The landscape features open fields, dense forests, and mountainous areas, including hills like Atalaya mountain and Navillas mountain. The Alhama river flows through the area, contributing to scenic routes. This variety provides suitable paths for different skill levels. Best hiking trails around Fitero The most popular hiking route is Las Roscas y Cueva de la Mora — circular desde Fitero, a 6.9 miles (11.0 km) trail that takes 3 hours 3 minutes to complete. This moderate circular route explores varied terrain with an elevation gain of almost 200 meters. Another top favourite among local hikers is Circuito de las Roscas – circular from Fitero via Camino del Soto, a moderate 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route offers a circular journey through the local landscape, passing via Camino del Soto. Local hikers also love the Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas — circular desde Fitero, a 8.9 miles (14.4 km) trail leading through barrancos and offering views towards Atalaya mountain, often completed in about 4 hours 6 minutes. Hiking around Fitero is defined by varied terrain, including open fields, dense forests, hills such as Atalaya and Navillas, with the Alhama river adding to the natural beauty. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging trails. The routes in Fitero are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 170 hikers have used komoot to explore Fitero's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hiking trails around Fitero?
A: Hiking around Fitero offers a diverse landscape. You'll encounter open fields, dense forests, and mountainous areas, including hills like Atalaya and Navillas. The Alhama River flows through the region, adding to the scenic beauty, and there are also beautiful pine groves, particularly near the Baños de Fitero spa. This varied topography provides a range of gradients and views.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or casual walkers in Fitero?
A: Yes, Fitero offers several routes suitable for easier walks. Out of the over a dozen hiking trails available, four are classified as easy, making them perfect for those looking for a more relaxed experience or shorter strolls through the natural surroundings.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Fitero?
A: Absolutely. Many of the trails in Fitero are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location without retracing your steps. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1242079108/las-roscas-y-cueva-de-la-mora-circular-desde-fitero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas y Cueva de la Mora — circular desde Fitero&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 11 km loop, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1245386190/barrancode-los-blancares-y-atalaya-de-navillas-circular-desde-fitero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas — circular desde Fitero&lt;/a&gt; offers a longer 14.4 km circular journey.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks to look out for while hiking in Fitero?
A: The region boasts several interesting natural features and landmarks. You can explore areas like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3598457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Roscas&lt;/a&gt;, a natural monument, or enjoy views from prominent peaks such as Atalaya de Fitero and Alto del Fraile. The Baños de Fitero spa area also features beautiful pine groves and an outdoor hydrothermal pool integrated into the natural setting.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Fitero?
A: The hiking trails in Fitero are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.5 stars from over 20 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, which includes open fields, dense forests, and scenic river views, making for a rewarding outdoor experience.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Fitero?
A: While specific family-friendly routes aren't explicitly highlighted, the presence of four easy-rated trails suggests there are options suitable for families with children or those preferring less strenuous walks. These routes typically feature gentler gradients and shorter distances, allowing for an enjoyable outing for all ages.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads in Fitero using public transport?
A: Information regarding specific public transport links directly to trailheads in Fitero is limited. It is generally recommended to have personal transportation to access the more remote starting points of hiking routes in the region. However, the town of Fitero itself may have local bus services.

Q: Where can I park my car when going for a hike in Fitero?
A: While specific parking locations for each trailhead are not detailed, Fitero is a municipality that accommodates visitors. You can typically find parking within the town of Fitero, and many trailheads on the outskirts of the town or near popular attractions often have designated or informal parking areas. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fitero?
A: Fitero's diverse landscapes can be enjoyed across multiple seasons. Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant scenery. Summer can be warm, but early mornings or late afternoons are still suitable. Winter hiking is possible, especially on lower elevation trails, but always check local weather conditions.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging hikes available in Fitero?
A: Yes, for those seeking a longer challenge, Fitero offers several moderate routes that cover significant distances and elevation. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1245368226/from-valverde-to-valdegutur-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Valverde to Valdegutur — circular&lt;/a&gt; route is a moderate 13 km hike with over 240 meters of elevation gain, providing a more extensive exploration of the area's varied terrain.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Fitero?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on hiking trails in natural areas like Fitero, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. It's always good practice to keep your dog on a leash and clean up after them to ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers and to protect the local environment.

Q: Are there any specific viewpoints or panoramic spots along the trails?
A: The varied topography of Fitero, with its mountains and valleys, naturally offers numerous viewpoints. Trails that ascend hills like Atalaya and Navillas, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1245386190/barrancode-los-blancares-y-atalaya-de-navillas-circular-desde-fitero" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas — circular desde Fitero&lt;/a&gt;, provide excellent opportunities for panoramic vistas of the surrounding Ribera de Navarra region.

At the top are the remains of an old castle. Spectacular views and easy access.

The cross takes its name from the ruins of an Arab defensive tower ("watchtower") that existed in the same place and now lies under the cement base of the Cross. It was one of the many defensive towers whose remains are repeated throughout the Alhama valley and which were reconquered by the Castilians in the 12th century. The current stone cross dates back to 1973, when the previous wooden one that existed since 1908 was replaced. An information panel with a large photo of the area explains the most emblematic places of Fitero, los Baños and their surroundings. -misescapadespornavarra-

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike the Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas circular route?
faqs.answer: This moderate circular hike usually takes around 4 hours and 6 minutes to complete, covering a distance of approximately 14.4 kilometers.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas trail?
faqs.answer: The trail begins in the upper part of Fitero. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, you can typically find parking within the village, especially near the starting point, which transitions from a street to a gravel track.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Rated as moderate, the trail involves some ascent but is not overly extreme. It's suitable for hikers with some experience. While not explicitly stated as 'family-friendly,' its moderate difficulty suggests it could be manageable for active families.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: The terrain varies, starting on a street in Fitero, transitioning to a gravel track, then ascending through fields and into pine forests. You'll also encounter almond groves, corrals, and a forest track along a ridge. A pedestrian promenade leads back to the village.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll encounter several notable points, including the unique geological formations of the Macizo de Roscas. From the Cruz de la Atalaya de Navillas, you'll get magnificent views of Fitero and the Alhama river valley. Other highlights include the Barranco de los Blancares, the historic Tudejen, and the Monasterio Santa María la Real in Fitero.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers beautiful scenery with almond trees and vineyards, suggesting spring (for blossoms) and autumn (for colors) would be particularly picturesque. The moderate difficulty makes it enjoyable across many seasons, but always check local weather conditions.

faqs.question: Is the Barrancode los Blancares y Atalaya de Navillas trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The trail passes through natural landscapes, including fields and forests. While not explicitly stated, most trails in such areas are dog-friendly, though it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially when passing through agricultural areas or near wildlife.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. It appears to be freely accessible.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ruta Saludable y Cultural del Alhama, Camino del Soto, and Camino de la Vega a Fitero.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities or amenities near the trailhead in Fitero?
faqs.answer: Fitero is a village, so you can expect to find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and potentially accommodation options, making it convenient for pre- or post-hike refreshments and stays.

faqs.question: What makes the Macizo de Roscas a significant feature on this trail?
faqs.answer: The Macizo de Roscas is a geomorphologically spectacular massif, formed by conglomerates with varied blocks and stones. Erosion has created picturesque and unique rock formations with alternating indentations and protrusions, whose appearance changes dramatically with the sun's position and lighting, making it a visual marvel.
